Hey, what's happening? Welcome back. All right. So I'm making this video for you on the evening of Saturday, August 28th. And I'm making this for you because if you've been following along with our training and doing everything like exactly in real time, that means that you started with your seed campaigns almost two weeks ago. So we started building hours, you know, when I recorded it on August 16th. So if you did it, you know, exactly day by day, just like we were doing in the class, then you've got one that's been running for about two weeks. And you've also started like kind of scaling things. So if you're like most people, some stuff worked and most didn't. And because it's been almost two weeks, you probably spent a little bit of money now. And because most stuff didn't work, it's very, very easy to let that weird you out and discourage you. So most of the time, that weirdness and that discouragement and potential overwhelm is not really merited. It's understandable, but actually it's just our brains freaking us out. Sometimes stuff just isn't working. So what I want to do in this video is give you a checklist and walk through some guiding principles with you that will really help you keep on track and most importantly, not get weirded out. And if something's broken, figure out what it really is. Because most of the time, when something's not working, and this is assuming that we even have enough data, but when something's not working, it's usually like one little thing. But our brain wants to tell us that, oh my gosh, we got to remake the entire freaking business or whatever. So I've created a diagnostic checklist for you. Yes, that sounds very exciting. And I'm going to walk you through it right now. So you should see it on your screen. I've got a PDF of this available for you as well. And first of all, here are some general principles. So I'm basing this on, we built these campaigns almost two weeks ago. And even if they're going great as a general principle, two weeks is just not enough time to draw any super strong conclusions from. So it's just too early. I'm going to fix that typo right now before I export this as PDF. And that's good or bad. So if the campaigns are going great, that doesn't mean you're set for life. And if they're going poorly, it doesn't mean you're doomed. It's only been two weeks. And the reason why this is true is because the less data, and by data that could be clicks to your websites or leads or whatever, the less data we have, the less strength that any assumption will have. So if you're like most people, you know, you didn't just like pour $1,000 a day into these campaigns or whatever, you're probably following along, you know, like we're doing in the training. So it's pretty unlikely you've spent a lot of money. And therefore, you probably haven't gotten a lot of clicks to your website. And you haven't gotten a lot of leads and you haven't gotten a lot of purchases. And that's okay. Because, you know, we haven't really spent a lot. But our brains, it's really easy for our brains to be like, Oh, my gosh, it's been two weeks, you know, but you always have to look at the data. All right. And the final little guiding principle here is we always want to use the proper diagnostic process to avoid any type of emotional reaction, whether that is good or bad. Okay, so what I want to do with you is I want to go through this checklist in case, you know, let's say you like literally nothing has worked. So generally, you know, we'll have something where we will test out 10 or 20 different adsets or whatever. And, you know, we're testing out 2017 of them might be terrible, but three of them are pretty good. And then we go on to scale those three, etc, etc, etc. But sometimes it's feasibly possible for none of them to work. So in the event something like that happens or in the event, you know, in general, when you have a campaign or something that's not doing right, this is the checklist to go through just to sort of find out what's going on and to prevent any unnecessary stress. All right. So thing number one is we need to make sure the campaign is set to a conversion's objective. And what that means, so let's say we've got a campaign that's just been, you know, not performing properly or whatever, and we'll go into the numbers of these here in a minute. The first thing you got to look at is just make sure right here that the campaign level click on edit and make sure this is actually says conversions. A lot of time people have like they'll just do it on the accident or maybe they weren't paying attention or whatever, or maybe they're trying to get creative and they'll say, oh, you know, I set this to traffic right here, you know, and essentially, if you do that, you'll get a ton of traffic, but nobody will convert. You're telling Facebook's algorithm to get you clickers and not converters. All right. So that's thing number one, always make sure that it's set to conversions. I can't tell you how many like near disastrous things have been avoided because early on we found out, oh my gosh, this was set to traffic or reach or something, it wasn't set to conversions. So sound simple, is simple, but much better to have a simple mistake than a complicated, difficult one. You know what I mean? Okay, onward and upward. So next thing is you want to make sure you're looking at your data at the ad set level and not at the campaign as a whole. All right. So let me show you what I mean. So I'm using high rows right here. All right. The list to save for right now, I'm not and we're looking at, let's see one that looks like it's really doing badly. This is this one where it's telling me, okay, I'm only getting a 0.5 row ass, which means I'm losing money. Right. So it means this campaign right here, according to Facebook stats, is losing money. And, you know, we don't want to do that. But remember, a campaign is typically made up of a bunch of different audiences and ad sets. So if you look at it at the campaign level, it's easy to get freaked out. But if we were to go into this campaign, we might see that, what do you know, some of these audiences are actually pretty good, like this one is good. And this one right here is, which one is there? There we go. Gary Vaynerchuk, my man. See, coming on for us here. So these two are great. All right. These two audiences are cool. We deserve eventually going to turn these guys off. So it's very, very easy to, you know, to miss out on that and understand that, hey, you know, when we're starting out with this stuff, most of these audiences won't work. And that's going to therefore make the campaign itself look terrible. But in reality, no, the campaign itself is not terrible. We've got some winners in here. And this is good. Okay, so let's look now at our third thing on the checklist, which is, are you sure that the results that Facebook is showing you is accurate? All right. And sometimes, well, you know, a lot of times they are not accurate. And we've discussed tracking and the importance of that before. And one of my favorite methods is a track is the old redneck tracking method, where you have a Facebook specific funnel set up, you know. So one thing we can do is I can look over here. So let's say I'm looking at Facebook for today. And it's saying I've spent 359 bucks and I've made $149. So that looks pretty bad. All right. I don't like the looks of that at all. So actually, this is slightly inaccurate. Let me get this to be a little bit better because I'm running other campaigns to that funnel besides this one. So let's just have a look here. Let me show you something kind of cool. If you ever want to really look at all your stuff in Facebook, you can filter particular things out by their name. So I'm going to get everything where the campaign name contains four, because all the campaigns I'm running that are advertising this particular product here. I have that naming convention. And let's just see here. Still, it's saying that I haven't made that much money. And of course, you got to love Facebook, you know. It's not showing me anything at the total. So I would have to manually add all of this kind of stuff up, which is great. So thanks, guys. This is awesome. So actually, let me take a minute and do that, right? Hang on. I don't want to make you watch me do it on camera. It'll be boring. There we go. Okay. Thank goodness for calculators on phones. Yeah. So it's telling me over here on your Facebooks that I spent 1600 bucks. And, you know, if I add all of these totals up, Facebook is telling me I've made 2344. Now, we talked about the redneck tracking method. And this is where that really comes in handy. So all of my Facebook traffic for this particular offer goes to its own funnel, which is this one right here. So it's actually Facebook's underreporting a little bit, you know, by a couple hundred bucks here. So that's always a possibility. So there's no need to do this on a daily basis. But before you get yourself weirded out, just make sure that, you know, what they're telling you is actually right. Okay. And then finally, make sure your text's working. Sometimes, you know, especially if you use sites like ClickFunnels or whatever, sometimes they just will mess up on you. I recently had a client who I hate to throw a rustle into the bus here, because I like ClickFunnels. They're a great company. But, you know, all software kind of gets weird sometimes. But her business was really struggling. And it turns out that her one-click upsells just did not work on ClickFunnels. Like it had just broken and sweet. She switched over to a different shopping cart system. And what do you know, the pages were identical and all that. It was literally just a different shopping cart system. And the one-click upsells started working and the business got back to normal. So you always want to make sure your tech is working. Okay. So assuming like all of that stuff is cool, right? Like, you know, you got your campaign set up properly and all that. And, you know, it's been enough time. You've got enough data to where you're like, okay, you know, we got to change some stuff around. Here's a checklist of what to look at and how to actually diagnose it.
